summary: Backend-only test pass for TV Stream API. 15/15 pytest tests passed after fixing a proxy bug. Verified Xtream endpoints (login/categories/streams) error contracts with fake creds, real M3U parse from iptv-org (categories+total>0), proxy_stream over external URL, favorites CRUD with no _id leakage and idempotent upsert, plus CORS.

critical.issue: Was returning 503 via ingress for non-m3u8 URLs. Root cause: code forwarded upstream Content-Length while httpx auto-decompressed gzip body -> h11 LocalProtocolError 'Too much data for declared Content-Length'. FIXED by removing content-length forwarding (Response sets it correctly).

minor.issue: Login uses 502 (not 401) when server is unreachable; that is acceptable but UI should map both to 'credenciales/servidor' message.
No request timeout-specific handling beyond shared 30s; large/long-lived HLS segments may time out.

Streams entire body into memory (upstream.content + Response). For VOD .ts segments this is fine but full VOD downloads through proxy will buffer in RAM. Consider StreamingResponse with httpx.stream() for large bodies.

action_items: Verify the proxy fix in /app/backend/server.py (content-length removed from forwarded headers).
Consider StreamingResponse + httpx.stream for large media segments to avoid loading bodies into memory.
Optionally normalize all Xtream upstream errors to 401 when user_info is missing (currently 502 for unreachable host).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py proxy forwarded content-length while httpx decompresses gzip — caused 503 via ingress (now fixed).
proxy_stream loads full upstream.content in memory; not suitable for video/segment streaming at scale.
Favorites endpoints correctly exclude _id via projection {_id:0} and Pydantic FavoriteItem — no ObjectId leakage observed.
Single shared httpx.AsyncClient with follow_redirects=True is good; shutdown handler closes it. OK.
CORS allow_origins from env defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject cookies in this combo. Acceptable for this app (no auth cookies) but worth flagging.
M3U parser reads entire body into memory; fine for typical playlists; iptv-org index.m3u parsed successfully (10000+ entries).
test_credentials: None required; favorites tested with TEST_-prefixed client_id (auto-cleaned).
seed_data_creation: Favorites docs created with client_id prefix TEST_<uuid8> and cleaned up via teardown fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green. Run `pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py` to regress. Proxy fix is the only behavioural change. For frontend testing next, use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. The /api/proxy/stream endpoint can be exercised with https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iptv-org/iptv/master/README.md as a safe small file.
rca_of_the_issue: Bug: GET /api/proxy/stream for non-m3u8 URLs returned 503 via the public ingress (200 sometimes locally). Reproduction: curl https://<public>/api/proxy/stream?u=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iptv-org/iptv/master/README.md -> 503; backend log showed 'h11._util.LocalProtocolError: Too much data for declared Content-Length'. RCA: in proxy_stream, the response forwarded the upstream Content-Length header. httpx auto-decompresses gzip, so upstream.content is the decompressed body whose length differs from the original Content-Length header. Starlette/h11 then raised when sending more bytes than the declared length. Mitigation: stop forwarding content-length (let Starlette compute it). Optionally use httpx.stream() with StreamingResponse for true pass-through.
